prompt: 2026 SEO + content-authority playbook for a technical B2B security product targeting 'AI agent security', 'AI agent security audit', 'signed security attestation', 'SOC 2 for AI agents', 'EU AI Act agent compliance', and procurement/security-review buyers. What technical SEO (Core Web Vitals, structured data/JSON-LD types: SoftwareApplication/Service/FAQ/HowTo, sitemap, canonical, metadata, OG), content (pillar pages, comparison pages, framework guides, the buyer's-questionnaire content, glossary), and authority signals (backlinks, G2/review sites, docs) actually move rankings + conversion for this category now? Which keywords have real intent + low competition for a new entrant. Cite specifics. Conclude with a prioritized SEO + content action list kolm should ship.
content:
**Technical SEO foundations that matter in 2026 for this B2B security SaaS category emphasize crawlability, speed/user experience signals, and entity-rich structured data to help Google (and AI search surfaces) understand your product as a credible solution for AI agent security, audits, attestations, SOC 2, and EU AI Act compliance.**[[1]](https://www.marketermilk.com/blog/saas-seo)[[2]](https://www.gravitatedesign.com/blog/b2b-saas-seo-strategies/)

**Core Web Vitals (CWV)** remain a confirmed ranking factor with direct conversion impact. Key 2026 thresholds (field data via CrUX): LCP ≤2.5s (some analyses note tighter effective scrutiny around 2.0s for competitive queries), INP ≤200ms (fully replaced FID as the responsiveness metric, measuring full interaction lifecycle), and CLS ≤0.1. Passing these improves rankings in close competitions and boosts conversions (e.g., faster loads can lift rates significantly for procurement pages). Monitor via Google PageSpeed Insights, Search Console, and tools like GTmetrix; prioritize mobile, compress media (WebP), optimize JS, and use CDNs/caching. Heavy SaaS dashboards or docs sites often need specific INP fixes for interactivity.[[3]](https://www.rivuletiq.com/core-web-vitals-2026-whats-changed-and-how-to-pass/)[[4]](https://whitelabelcoders.com/blog/how-important-are-core-web-vitals-for-seo-in-2026/)[[5]](https://www.interactmarketing.com/why-core-web-vitals-still-matter-more-than-you-think-in-2026/)

**Structured data/JSON-LD** (Google's recommended format) is repeatedly highlighted for SaaS to enable rich results and better entity understanding. Prioritize:
- **SoftwareApplication** (or WebApplication subtype) on product/home pages: Include name, applicationCategory (e.g., "SecurityApplication"), featureList, operatingSystem ("Web"), offers, and aggregateRating for trust signals.[[6]](https://iloveschema.com/software-application-schema-generator/)[[7]](https://salt.agency/blog/schema-for-saas-companies-salt-agency/)[[8]](https://singularity.digital/insights/what-schema-markup-is-and-how-anyone-can-implement-it-on-saas-websites/)
- **Service** on relevant service/compliance pages.
- **FAQPage** for common buyer questions (e.g., "How does this support SOC 2 for AI agents?") to enable expandable rich results.
- **HowTo** is largely deprecated for rich results (step carousels no longer shown since ~2023), so use sparingly or avoid relying on it for visibility.[[9]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/structured-data-seo-2026-rich-results-guide)

Implement site-wide via JSON-LD in `<head>` or body; validate with Google's Rich Results Test. Combine with **canonical tags** (self-referential on primary pages; point duplicates to canonical), clean XML sitemaps (include all priority pages like pillars and docs), descriptive metadata (title ~50-60 chars with primary keywords, meta description with CTAs/intent match), and Open Graph (OG) tags for social amplification of comparison/framework content. Ensure HTTPS, robots.txt optimization for crawl budget, and noindex on thin/internal pages.[[1]](https://www.marketermilk.com/blog/saas-seo)[[10]](https://www.tripledart.com/saas-seo/technical-seo)

**Content strategy** should target high-intent procurement/security-review buyers with educational, comparison-driven, and compliance-focused assets that signal category leadership. Focus on non-generic, in-depth insights rather than broad traffic plays.[[11]](https://overthinkgroup.com/b2b-seo/)

- **Pillar pages**: Comprehensive hubs on "AI Agent Security" (threats like prompt injection/tool poisoning/shadow agents, defenses, governance) and "AI Agent Security Audit" (frameworks, checklists, attestation processes). Link to supporting clusters.
- **Comparison pages**: "SOC 2 for AI Agents vs Traditional SOC 2," vendor comparisons, or "Signed Security Attestation: Our Approach vs. Alternatives."
- **Framework guides**: EU AI Act agent compliance playbooks, risk assessment frameworks, or "Buyer's Guide to AI Agent Security Attestations."
- **Buyer's questionnaire content**: Interactive or downloadable self-assessments ("Security Review Questionnaire for AI Agent Vendors") that capture leads while educating on requirements (identity, runtime controls, audit trails, compliance mappings).
- **Glossary**: Authoritative terms page defining "AI agent security," "signed security attestation," "agentic threats," etc., with internal links; aids E-E-A-T and featured snippets.

Emphasize original research, real buyer pain points (e.g., adoption outpacing governance, per 2026 reports showing low full-approval rates), and conversion paths (demos, RFPs, questionnaires). Update regularly for freshness.[[12]](https://www.gravitee.io/blog/state-of-ai-agent-security-2026-report-when-adoption-outpaces-control)

**Authority signals** that move rankings and conversions include:
- **Backlinks** from security/tech publications, compliance sites, analyst reports, and relevant forums (prioritize quality/contextual over volume).
- **G2/Capterra/review sites**: Encourage verified reviews highlighting compliance features, audits, and buyer outcomes; these provide social proof and structured data fodder.
- **Docs/public resources**: Comprehensive, crawlable documentation on implementation, APIs, compliance mappings, and attestations (builds trust for technical buyers and supports internal linking).
- Additional signals: Mentions in industry reports/summits (e.g., AI Agent Security events), case studies with procurement outcomes, and partnerships or certifications (SOC 2, etc.).

These help with E-E-A-T for a new entrant in an emerging category.[[13]](https://cuttingedgepr.com/articles/6-best-b2b-saas-seo-agencies-in-2026-a-brief-overview/)

**Keywords with real intent + low competition for a new entrant**: The category is nascent and growing rapidly (2026 reports, summits, tools, and frameworks indicate rising demand), but not yet saturated like general "AI security." High commercial/procurement intent exists around compliance, audits, and attestations—buyers (security teams, CISOs, procurement) search for solutions addressing governance gaps, regulatory needs (EU AI Act), and verifiable controls (SOC 2, signed attestations). Exact search volumes are not publicly detailed in results, but niche long-tails show lower competition than broad terms.[[12]](https://www.gravitee.io/blog/state-of-ai-agent-security-2026-report-when-adoption-outpaces-control)[[14]](https://zenity.io/resources/events/ai-agent-security-summit-2026)

Promising targets (real buyer intent, relatively low comp for new players):
- "AI agent security audit"
- "signed security attestation"
- "SOC 2 for AI agents"
- "EU AI Act agent compliance"
- Related long-tails: "AI agent security framework," "agentic AI compliance checklist," "AI agent attestation requirements," or "securing AI agents SOC 2."

Broader "AI agent security" has emerging content but room for differentiation via depth/compliance focus. Prioritize intent-matching over volume; these convert via trust and proof points.

**Prioritized SEO + content action list (ship in this order for fastest impact)**:
1. **Technical baseline (Weeks 1-4)**: Implement sitemap, canonicals, metadata/OG, core schema (SoftwareApplication + FAQ on key pages), CWV optimizations, and HTTPS/mobile fixes. Audit with Screaming Frog/PageSpeed tools.
2. **Keyword mapping & on-page (parallel, Weeks 1-6)**: Map targets to pillars/comparisons; optimize existing or new pages with primary keywords in titles/H1s/naturally.
3. **Core content launch (Weeks 4-12)**: Publish 1-2 pillar pages, glossary, and 1-2 framework/comparison pieces; create buyer's questionnaire.
4. **Authority build (ongoing, start Week 4+)**: Seed reviews on G2, pursue relevant backlinks/PR, publish docs, and engage in industry discussions/reports.
5. **Measurement & iteration (ongoing)**: Track CWV in GSC, rankings/traffic for target keywords, conversions (form fills, demo requests), and AI/search visibility. Refresh content quarterly.

This playbook focuses on what drives both rankings (technical + E-E-A-T) and conversions (intent-aligned, trust-building content) in a high-stakes B2B security niche. Execute fundamentals first for compounding gains.
